steps.title: Identify the slope
steps.content: The equation $l = 25 + 3w$ is linear in slope-intercept form. The 3 is the coefficient of $w$, so it is the slope of $l$ versus $w$.

steps.title: Interpret the slope in context
steps.content: Slope = (change in dependent variable) / (change in independent variable). Here $l$ (length, cm) depends on $w$ (weight, N), so the 3 means $l$ increases by 3 cm for each 1 N increase in $w$.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A describes the value 25, the length with no weight attached.
  • Choice B also refers to the starting length of 25 centimeters, not the rate of change.
  • Choice C reverses the relationship by describing weight per centimeter of length.
  • The increase in the length, in centimeters, of the spring for each one-newton increase in the weight of the object is choice D.
